channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: CATS Fellowships 2026/2027 in Berlin, Germany (Fully Funded): Monthly €3,000 Stipend, Travel Support & Global Eligibility
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The CATS Fellowships 2026/2027 in Berlin, Germany is now open for applications. This prestigious fully funded fellowship is designed for researchers, policy experts, think tank professionals, and practitioners working on Turkey-related issues. Hosted by the Centre for Applied Turkey Studies (CATS) at the German Institute for International and Security Affairs (SWP), the fellowship offers a […]

The post CATS Fellowships 2026/2027 in Berlin, Germany (Fully Funded): Monthly €3,000 Stipend, Travel Support & Global Eligibility appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The CATS Fellowships 2026/2027 in Berlin, Germany is now open for applications. This prestigious fully funded fellowship is designed for researchers, policy experts, think tank professionals, and practitioners working on Turkey-related issues. Hosted by the Centre for Applied Turkey Studies (CATS) at the German Institute for International and Security Affairs (SWP), the fellowship offers a unique opportunity to conduct impactful research in one of Europe’s leading academic and policy environments.

Applicants from all countries can apply, and there is no application fee.

The fellowship runs for 6 to 12 months, beginning from September 2026, and selected fellows will be based in Berlin, Germany.

Why Apply for the CATS Fellowship?

This program is ideal for scholars and professionals seeking to deepen their expertise in Turkish politics, security, climate policy, digital transformation, and geopolitical affairs while collaborating with leading researchers in Germany.

Selected fellows will gain access to a world-class research network, publication opportunities, and financial support to focus fully on their projects.

Fellowship Benefits

The CATS Fellowship is fully funded and includes:

  • Monthly grant of €3,000
  • Travel allowance to and from Berlin
  • Budget for research trips
  • Tuition support
  • Participation in field trips
  • Additional family support for fellows accompanied by children
  • Collaboration opportunities with CATS and SWP researchers
  • Possibility of publishing research in peer-reviewed CATS and SWP outlets

Research Topics for 2026/2027

Applicants are invited to submit proposals under the following themes:

1. Digitalisation and Artificial Intelligence

Research may explore Turkey’s AI strategy, digital governance, cyber resilience, defense modernization, labor markets, and economic transformation through emerging technologies.

2. Critical Raw Materials (CRMs)

Topics include Turkey’s rare earth minerals strategy, extraction policies, geopolitical competition, industrial upgrading, and EU-Turkey cooperation on critical resources.

3. Energy Transition and Climate Change

Applicants may focus on Turkey’s climate commitments, water scarcity, renewable energy strategy, governance reforms, and environmental security.

4. Turkish Foreign and Security Policy Amid Geopolitical Turmoil

Areas include NATO dynamics, Black Sea security, South Caucasus relations, regional power competition, maritime strategy, and energy corridors.

Who Should Apply?

This fellowship is suitable for:

  • Postdoctoral researchers
  • Academics and scholars
  • Think tank analysts
  • Policy experts
  • Practitioners in relevant sectors
  • Researchers with strong publication records
  • PhD candidates with proven expertise in Turkey-related research

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit one single PDF file containing:

  • Cover letter
  • Research proposal (maximum 5 pages)
  • Research objectives and relevance
  • State of research overview
  • Methodology
  • Bibliography
  • CV with list of publications
  • Academic transcripts and/or diplomas

Applications may be submitted in English or German.

Selection Criteria

Applications will be assessed based on:

  • Academic and professional merit
  • Relevance to fellowship themes
  • Feasibility and coherence of proposal
  • Public outreach value of the project

Preference may be given to applicants who have not previously received CATS funding in cases of equal merit.

Deadline

The application deadline is 30 April 2026. Late or incomplete applications will not be considered.

How to Apply

Send your complete application as a single PDF document via email to:

CATSapplication@swp-berlin.org

For inquiries: CATS@swp-berlin.org

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here. 

Final Thoughts

If you are a researcher or policy professional looking for a fully funded fellowship in Europe, the CATS Fellowships 2026/2027 in Germany is an outstanding opportunity. With generous funding, access to influential networks, and a chance to contribute to high-level policy research, this fellowship can significantly advance your academic and professional career.

The post CATS Fellowships 2026/2027 in Berlin, Germany (Fully Funded): Monthly €3,000 Stipend, Travel Support & Global Eligibility appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Become a Mentor in Europe’s Leading Women Innovation Programme: Apply for the EIC Women Leadership Programme 2026
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The European Innovation Council (EIC) has officially opened applications for mentors to join the prestigious EIC Women Leadership Programme 2026. This is a unique opportunity for experienced leaders across Europe’s innovation ecosystem to guide and empower the next generation of women innovators, researchers, and entrepreneurs. Running from May to November 2026, the programme connects accomplished […]

The post Become a Mentor in Europe’s Leading Women Innovation Programme: Apply for the EIC Women Leadership Programme 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The European Innovation Council (EIC) has officially opened applications for mentors to join the prestigious EIC Women Leadership Programme 2026. This is a unique opportunity for experienced leaders across Europe’s innovation ecosystem to guide and empower the next generation of women innovators, researchers, and entrepreneurs.

Running from May to November 2026, the programme connects accomplished mentors with high-potential women building breakthrough technologies and ambitious ventures. If you are passionate about leadership, innovation, and creating impact, this mentor call offers a chance to shape the future of European entrepreneurship.

What is the EIC Women Leadership Programme?

The EIC Women Leadership Programme was created to support women researchers and entrepreneurs by strengthening leadership skills, expanding networks, and increasing visibility within Europe’s innovation ecosystem.

Since its pilot launch in 2021, the initiative has supported more than 300 women innovators through business coaching, personal mentoring, leadership training, and networking opportunities.

A core feature of the programme is its structured mentoring scheme, where experienced professionals help participants grow as leaders and scale their ventures.

Why Become a Mentor?

Mentors contribute on a pro bono basis while gaining access to one of Europe’s most valuable innovation networks.

Key Benefits for Mentors

  • Official mentor recognition and certification
  • Increased visibility through EIC programme channels
  • Inclusion in the mentors’ brochure
  • Access to an exclusive network of investors, founders, and innovation leaders
  • Speaking and thought leadership opportunities
  • Exposure to cutting-edge startups and emerging technologies
  • Opportunity to shape women’s leadership in Europe
  • Possibility to attend the EIC Summit
  • Invitations to networking events within the EIC ecosystem

What Does a Mentor Do?

Mentors support selected participants by sharing expertise, strategic guidance, and professional networks.

The mentoring relationship focuses on:

  • Leadership development and confident decision-making
  • Strategic thinking in leadership roles
  • Career growth and executive positioning
  • Expanding networks and increasing visibility
  • Scaling ventures and overcoming business challenges

Mentors are carefully matched with mentees based on expertise, experience, and participant needs.

Mentor Journey and Timeline

The programme is designed to be efficient, structured, and respectful of mentors’ schedules.

Important Dates

  • 13 May 2026: Online onboarding workshop
  • 30 June 2026: Mentor-mentee matching confirmed
  • July – November 2026: Mentoring sessions and networking activities
  • 27 November 2026: Final Programme Event, Demo Day, and Pitching Session

Time Commitment

Mentors will participate in:

  • 5–6 online mentoring sessions
  • Approximately 1 hour per session
  • Flexible scheduling based on availability

Who Should Apply?

This opportunity is ideal for professionals who want to create meaningful impact in Europe’s innovation ecosystem, including:

  • Startup founders
  • Entrepreneurs
  • Angel investors
  • Venture capital partners
  • Senior executives
  • Innovation consultants
  • Ecosystem leaders

How to Apply

Interested applicants can submit their application through the official application form provided by the European Innovation Council.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here. 

Application Deadline

30 April 2026 (End of Business Day)

Early applications are strongly recommended.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Women innovators continue to transform industries across Europe. By becoming a mentor in the EIC Women Leadership Programme, you can directly contribute to a more inclusive and competitive innovation landscape while expanding your own influence and network.

This is more than mentorship—it is a chance to help build the future of European leadership and entrepreneurship.

The post Become a Mentor in Europe’s Leading Women Innovation Programme: Apply for the EIC Women Leadership Programme 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Top Free UN Online Courses 2026: Advance Your Career in Climate, Health, Sustainability & Global Development (With Certificates)
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
In today’s rapidly evolving world, gaining relevant, future-focused skills is no longer optional—it is essential. The United Nations, through platforms such as UN SDG: Learn, UNITAR, UNICEF, UNESCO, and the World Bank Group, is offering a wide range of free, self-paced online courses designed to equip learners with practical knowledge and globally relevant competencies. These […]

The post Top Free UN Online Courses 2026: Advance Your Career in Climate, Health, Sustainability & Global Development (With Certificates) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
In today’s rapidly evolving world, gaining relevant, future-focused skills is no longer optional—it is essential. The United Nations, through platforms such as UN SDG: Learn, UNITAR, UNICEF, UNESCO, and the World Bank Group, is offering a wide range of free, self-paced online courses designed to equip learners with practical knowledge and globally relevant competencies.

These courses cover critical areas such as climate action, global health, gender equality, sustainable lifestyles, mental health, and development financing. They are accessible to learners worldwide, require flexible time commitments, and many provide certificates upon completion.

Whether you are a student, professional, policymaker, or development practitioner, these programs provide an opportunity to build skills aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

Comprehensive List of Free UN Online Courses (2026)

1. Sports for Climate Action (Advanced Course)

This advanced-level course explores how the sports sector can contribute to combating climate change through sustainability strategies and emission reduction efforts.

Key Details

  • Level: Advanced
  • Duration: 4 hours
  • Format: Self-paced
  • Certificate: Yes
  • Language: English, Spanish

What You Will Learn

  • Climate mitigation and adaptation strategies in sports
  • Sustainable supply chains in sports organizations
  • Stakeholder engagement for climate action
  • Monitoring and reporting sustainability progress

Course Modules

  1. Winning the Climate Game
  2. The Game Plan for Green Sports
  3. Sustainable Supply Chains
  4. Engagement and Impact
  5. Tracking Progress

Learn More

2. Sustainable Lifestyles (Introductory Course)

This course focuses on how individual and collective lifestyle choices impact sustainability and how to transition toward responsible consumption patterns.

Key Details

  • Level: Introductory
  • Duration: 3 hours
  • Certificate: Yes

Core Learning Areas

  • Understanding sustainable consumption patterns
  • Lifestyle impacts on the environment
  • Policy frameworks supporting sustainability
  • Practical solutions tailored to different regions

Learn More

3. Adolescent Girls’ Agency, Safety and Wellbeing

This learning pathway focuses on gender-transformative approaches and improving outcomes for adolescent girls globally.

Key Details

  • Level: Introductory
  • Duration: 2.5 hours
  • Certificate: Yes

Learning Outcomes

  • Gender-transformative programming strategies
  • Ending child marriage and FGM
  • Socio-ecological model application
  • Empowerment and inclusion of vulnerable girls

Learn More

4. One Health in Practice: Ecosystems & Human Health

A comprehensive course exploring the link between biodiversity, ecosystems, and human health.

Key Details

  • Level: Intermediate
  • Duration: 10 weeks
  • Effort: 2–3 hours/week
  • Certificate: No

Key Topics

  • Zoonotic diseases and pandemics
  • Environmental determinants of health
  • Ecosystem resilience frameworks
  • Governance for health and biodiversity

Learn More

5. Mental Health and Psychosocial Support

A microlearning course focused on mental health support in conflict and post-conflict settings.

Key Details

  • Duration: 1 hour
  • Certificate: No

What You Will Gain

  • Understanding psychosocial wellbeing
  • Tools for community support
  • Conflict-sensitive mental health strategies

Learn More

6. Global Public Health

This course provides a deep dive into global health systems and their connection to development.

Key Details

  • Duration: 10 weeks
  • Effort: 4–6 hours/week
  • Certificate: No

Learning Focus

  • Global health systems and infrastructure
  • Impact of environment, migration, and trade
  • Health as a driver of development

Learn More

7. Measles Control in Emergencies

A specialized course focusing on outbreak prevention and response in humanitarian contexts.

Key Details

  • Duration: 2 hours
  • Certificate: Yes

Key Learning Areas

  • Risk assessment in emergencies
  • Outbreak investigation
  • Immunization strategies

Learn More

8. Communication for Immunization

A practical course on designing communication strategies to improve vaccination uptake.

Key Details

  • Duration: 3 hours
  • Certificate: Yes

Key Skills Developed

  • Behaviour change communication
  • Campaign planning and evaluation
  • Data-driven decision-making

Learn More

9. Results-Based Financing in Health

This course explores innovative financing models that improve healthcare outcomes.

Key Details

  • Duration: 5 weeks
  • Effort: 3–4 hours/week
  • Certificate: Yes

What You Will Learn

  • Principles of results-based financing (RBF)
  • Health system strengthening strategies
  • Policy and implementation frameworks

Learn More

10. Inner Development Goals for Sustainable Future (Podcast Learning)

This learning resource explores the importance of internal human capacities in achieving sustainability.

Key Insights

  • Emotional intelligence and leadership
  • Mindfulness and resilience
  • Collaboration and systemic change
  • Role of inner transformation in global development

Learn More

Why You Should Take These UN Courses

These programs provide:

  • Free access to high-quality UN learning resources
  • Globally recognized certificates (for selected courses)
  • Flexible, self-paced learning schedules
  • Practical skills aligned with SDGs
  • Exposure to global case studies and expert insights

How to Apply

  1. Visit the UN SDG: Learn platform
  2. Select your preferred course
  3. Register for free
  4. Start learning immediately

There is no strict deadline for most courses as they are self-paced, but early enrollment is encouraged.

Discover more global opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Top Free UN Online Courses 2026: Advance Your Career in Climate, Health, Sustainability & Global Development (With Certificates) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Youth Will LEAD Fellowship 2026 in San Diego, USA | $1,500 Paid Fellowship for Young Changemakers
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Youth Will LEAD Fellowship 2026, an exciting paid leadership opportunity for young people in San Diego County who are passionate about advocacy, youth empowerment, and creating change in their communities. The Youth Will LEAD Fellowship—short for Leadership, Empowerment, Advocacy, & Development—is a six-week summer fellowship designed to help young […]

The post Youth Will LEAD Fellowship 2026 in San Diego, USA | $1,500 Paid Fellowship for Young Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Youth Will LEAD Fellowship 2026, an exciting paid leadership opportunity for young people in San Diego County who are passionate about advocacy, youth empowerment, and creating change in their communities.

The Youth Will LEAD Fellowship—short for Leadership, Empowerment, Advocacy, & Development—is a six-week summer fellowship designed to help young leaders gain the knowledge, confidence, and practical skills needed to challenge outdated systems and build better futures for youth.

Selected fellows will receive a $1,500 stipend in recognition of their time, expertise, and commitment.

About the Youth Will LEAD Fellowship 2026

Youth Will believes young people deserve the freedom to pursue careers and life paths that bring joy, fulfillment, and long-term stability. Traditional career pathways often leave young people burdened by debt, stress, and jobs that do not align with their passions.

Through this fellowship, participants will explore alternative pathways, accessible education, policy advocacy, and youth wellbeing while helping shape conversations around education and workforce development.

Fellowship Benefits

Participants in the Youth Will LEAD Fellowship 2026 will receive:

  • $1,500 stipend for participation
  • Leadership development opportunities
  • Advocacy and policy engagement experience
  • Political analysis and historical context training
  • Research and survey development skills
  • Networking with like-minded young leaders
  • Real-world experience influencing youth-focused narratives
  • A supportive environment focused on mental health and wellbeing

Fellowship Focus Areas

Leadership Development

Build the confidence and skills to become an effective community advocate and changemaker.

Empowerment Through Education

Learn about alternative career pathways and accessible opportunities beyond traditional systems.

Advocacy Skills

Gain hands-on experience in policy discussions, research, and community engagement.

Mental Health & Wellbeing

Promote healthier pathways for youth by challenging pressure-filled and debt-heavy career expectations.

Fellowship Timeline

The fellowship runs for six weeks during the summer of 2026.

In-Person Session Dates

  • June 13
  • June 20
  • June 27
  • July 11
  • July 18
  • July 25

Session Time

10:00 AM – 2:30 PM (In Person)

Eligibility Criteria

To apply, applicants must:

  • Be 18–25 years old at the time of the fellowship
  • Be based in San Diego County
  • Be available to attend all fellowship session dates
  • Have an interest in leadership, advocacy, youth wellbeing, and community impact

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the Youth Will LEAD Fellowship 2026 is:

April 26, 2026 at 11:59 PM

Late applications may not be considered, so interested applicants should apply early.

How to Apply

Applicants can learn more and submit their application through the official website:

For questions or additional support, applicants can contact: hello@youthwill.org

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here. 

Why You Should Apply

If you are a young person ready to make an impact, grow as a leader, and help shape better opportunities for future generations, the Youth Will LEAD Fellowship 2026 is a valuable chance to turn your passion into action while earning financial support.

This fellowship is ideal for youth who want to gain real advocacy experience, expand their network, and contribute to meaningful change in their community.

The post Youth Will LEAD Fellowship 2026 in San Diego, USA | $1,500 Paid Fellowship for Young Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: RCDIJ Africa Fund 2026: Fully Funded Grant for African Journalists (Up to $10,000)
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
The Ripples Centre for Data and Investigative Journalism Africa Fund is one of the most impactful funding opportunities currently available for journalists across Africa. Designed to strengthen investigative reporting and data-driven storytelling, this grant provides financial and editorial support to journalists working on critical issues affecting the continent. About the RCDIJ Africa Fund The fund […]

The post RCDIJ Africa Fund 2026: Fully Funded Grant for African Journalists (Up to $10,000) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Ripples Centre for Data and Investigative Journalism Africa Fund is one of the most impactful funding opportunities currently available for journalists across Africa. Designed to strengthen investigative reporting and data-driven storytelling, this grant provides financial and editorial support to journalists working on critical issues affecting the continent.

About the RCDIJ Africa Fund

The fund is managed by the Ripples Centre for Data and Investigative Journalism (RCDIJ), an independent, non-profit media organization committed to promoting accountability, transparency, and democratic values through investigative journalism.

RCDIJ’s mission focuses on:

  • Exposing corruption and systemic failures
  • Strengthening democratic processes
  • Advancing data journalism across Africa
  • Supporting impactful storytelling that influences policy and society

Through this fund, RCDIJ empowers journalists to produce high-quality investigative work that addresses pressing challenges across the continent.

Funding Details and Benefits

The RCDIJ Africa Fund provides both financial and professional support, making it a highly competitive and valuable opportunity.

Financial Support

  • Grants range from under $1,000 to $10,000 depending on project scope
  • Funding is determined by:
    • Project scale
    • Geographic coverage
    • Duration of investigation

Additional Benefits

  • Editorial mentorship and guidance throughout the project
  • Opportunity for publication on Ripples Nigeria, RCDIJ’s media platform
  • Increased visibility for your investigative work
  • Strengthening of your professional journalism portfolio

Thematic Areas Covered

The fund supports investigative journalism projects across a wide range of sectors, including:

  • Education
  • Health
  • Security
  • Environment and climate change
  • Democracy and governance
  • Corruption and accountability
  • Economy and finance
  • Migration
  • Technology
  • Culture and development

These themes reflect Africa’s most pressing challenges and opportunities for transformation.

Who Can Apply?

Eligible Category of People

This opportunity is specifically designed for:

  • Individual journalists across Africa

Eligible applicants include:

  • Employed journalists
  • Freelance journalists
  • Online journalists
  • Print media professionals
  • Broadcast journalists
  • Photojournalists
  • Cartoonists
  • Multimedia journalists

Eligible Countries and Regions

  • Continent: Africa
  • Eligible Applicants: Journalists from all African countries

This makes the RCDIJ Africa Fund a pan-African opportunity, open to applicants from East, West, Central, North, and Southern Africa.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the RCDIJ Africa Fund, applicants must:

  • Be an individual journalist based in Africa
  • Have a strong interest in investigative or data journalism
  • Demonstrate prior work through published articles or media content
  • Submit a well-structured investigative project proposal
  • Be able to complete the project within the proposed timeline

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit a single PDF document (in English) containing:

  1. Project idea
  2. Detailed work plan
  3. Budget breakdown
  4. Links to previous published works
  5. Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  6. Data page of an international passport

How to Apply

Applicants can apply through either of the following methods:

  • Complete the official application form
  • Or send the required PDF document via email

Email Submission Details:

  • Email: info@rcdij.org
  • Subject Line: RCDIJ AFRICA FUND APPLICATION

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and applicants are typically notified within about two months after submission.

Apply here

Why You Should Apply for the RCDIJ Africa Fund

The RCDIJ Africa Fund stands out among journalism grants for several reasons:

1. Strong Financial Backing – With funding up to $10,000, it enables journalists to undertake ambitious investigative projects.

2. Editorial Support – Unlike many grants, this opportunity includes mentorship and editorial guidance.

3. Publication Opportunity – Your work may be featured on a recognized platform, increasing reach and credibility.

4. Pan-African Inclusion – Open to journalists from all African countries, ensuring diversity and inclusivity.

5. Impact-Driven Focus – Projects are designed to influence public discourse and policy.

Tips for a Strong Application

To increase your chances of success:

  • Choose a compelling, high-impact topic
  • Ensure your proposal is data-driven and evidence-based
  • Present a clear methodology and timeline
  • Provide a realistic and detailed budget
  • Showcase strong previous work samples
  • Focus on originality and investigative depth

Deadline

Ongoing

The RCDIJ Africa Fund is more than just a grant—it is a platform for African journalists to tell powerful stories, uncover hidden truths, and drive meaningful change across the continent. With an ongoing deadline, this opportunity allows you to apply at any time, making it accessible and flexible for journalists at different stages of their careers.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post RCDIJ Africa Fund 2026: Fully Funded Grant for African Journalists (Up to $10,000) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: World Diplomacy Forum Bali 2026: Fully Immersive Leadership Experience with 100+ Scholarships for Global Youth Leaders
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The World Diplomacy Forum (WDF) Bali 2026 is a highly immersive, action-driven global leadership experience designed for emerging leaders, diplomats, and changemakers who are ready to test their abilities in real-world scenarios. Taking place in Bali, Indonesia, this unique three-day forum goes far beyond a traditional conference. It places participants directly into high-pressure, real-time simulations […]

The post World Diplomacy Forum Bali 2026: Fully Immersive Leadership Experience with 100+ Scholarships for Global Youth Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The World Diplomacy Forum (WDF) Bali 2026 is a highly immersive, action-driven global leadership experience designed for emerging leaders, diplomats, and changemakers who are ready to test their abilities in real-world scenarios.

Taking place in Bali, Indonesia, this unique three-day forum goes far beyond a traditional conference. It places participants directly into high-pressure, real-time simulations involving diplomacy, crisis management, negotiation, and strategic decision-making.

Organized by the International School of Leadership and Diplomacy (ISLD), the program is built to develop the next generation of global leaders through experiential learning, global collaboration, and performance-based recognition.

Event Date and Location

  • Dates: August 7–9, 2026
  • Location: Bali, Indonesia

Application Process and Timeline

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, meaning early applicants have a higher chance of securing a spot and accessing scholarship opportunities.

Participants are encouraged to apply as early as possible due to limited slots and competitive scholarship allocation.

What is the World Diplomacy Forum?

The World Diplomacy Forum is not a conventional training or lecture-based program. Instead, it is a:

  • Real-time leadership simulation experience
  • High-pressure decision-making environment
  • Interactive diplomacy and negotiation platform

Participants are placed in dynamic global scenarios where they must:

  • Lead negotiations
  • Develop policy responses
  • Collaborate across cultures
  • Present strategic solutions under time constraints

The program is designed to transform how participants perceive leadership, influence, and global engagement.

Core Experience: What Participants Will Do

1. Lead in Real-Time Scenarios

Participants are immersed in situations where their leadership decisions directly influence outcomes.

2. Solve a Global AI Governance Crisis

  • Delegates are given 100 minutes to:
    • Research a crisis
    • Develop a policy response
    • Present their solution

3. Compete in “Last Leader Standing”

  • A high-stakes competition testing:
    • Communication
    • Influence
    • Confidence
    • Leadership presence

Core Leadership Themes

The program is structured around critical leadership competencies, including:

  • Strategic decision-making under pressure
  • AI governance and policy thinking
  • Diplomatic negotiation and influence
  • Crisis communication
  • Leadership presence and personal impact

These themes are integrated into all simulations, exercises, and evaluations.

Awards and Recognition

Participants are assessed continuously throughout the program by professional facilitators.

Key Awards Include:

  1. Most Influential Delegate
    • The highest individual honor awarded for leadership and influence
  2. Master of Crisis Leadership
    • Recognizes the best-performing team during crisis simulations
  3. Aspiring Leader Award
    • Honors the most improved participant
  4. Diplomatic Impact Award
    • Recognizes excellence in diplomacy and negotiation

Grand Privilege

  • Award winners receive:
    • Free access to the Leaders Summit USA 2027
  • All participants receive:
    • 50% discount on the Leaders Summit USA 2027

Benefits of Attending

Participants gain far more than theoretical knowledge.

Key Benefits:

  • Practical leadership experience in high-pressure scenarios
  • Global network of peers from 50+ countries
  • Official ISLD Certificate of Participation
  • Exposure to real-world diplomacy simulations
  • Access to exclusive global leadership events

Additional Value:

  • Bali cultural experience included in the program
  • Recognition that enhances career and academic profiles
  • Opportunities for long-term collaboration and networking

Scholarship Opportunities

The forum offers more than 100 scholarships to ensure accessibility for talented individuals worldwide.

Scholarship Model: Performance-Based

Applicants must complete a leadership assessment quiz after applying.

Scholarship Tiers:

  • 90–100: Presidential Scholar
    • Full program fee waiver (with interview)
  • 80–89: Excellence Scholar
    • 50% fee waiver
  • 65–79: Merit Scholar
    • 40% fee waiver
  • 50–64: Access Scholar
    • 30% fee waiver
  • Below 50: No scholarship

Important Notes:

  • Scholarships are awarded automatically based on quiz scores
  • No separate scholarship application is required
  • Offers are valid for 72 hours
  • Limited slots per tier

Programme Packages

Full Experience Package ($849 USD)

Includes:

  • Full 3-day program
  • 3 nights accommodation (twin-sharing)
  • Meals and networking events
  • Bali cultural experience
  • Delegate kit and certificate
  • Award eligibility

Register Now

Programme Only Package ($649 USD)

Includes:

  • Full program access
  • Meals and networking sessions
  • Certificate and materials
  • Bali experience
  • No accommodation included

Register Now

Why You Should Apply

The World Diplomacy Forum Bali 2026 offers a rare opportunity to:

  • Experience leadership under real pressure
  • Build global influence and confidence
  • Engage with diverse international peers
  • Gain recognition through performance-based awards
  • Join a global leadership network

This is not just a forum—it is a transformational leadership journey.

Application Process

The application process is structured and competitive:

  1. Submit application and pay commitment fee ($15.99)
  2. Complete leadership assessment quiz
  3. Receive scholarship decision
  4. Accept offer within 72 hours
  5. Receive official invitation letter
  6. Attend mandatory pre-event orientation
  7. Prepare and travel to Bali

Click HERE to apply

For more information about the World Diplomacy Forum (WDF) Bali 2026, visit HERE.

Discover more global Conference, Fellowship, Leadership and Scholarship opportunities on OFY.

The post World Diplomacy Forum Bali 2026: Fully Immersive Leadership Experience with 100+ Scholarships for Global Youth Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNIDO Project Associate (IFTI) 2026 in Vienna: Paid UN Opportunity for Young Professionals from the Global South
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The UNIDO Project Associate – Industrial Futures Talent Initiative (IFTI) 2026 is a highly competitive early-career opportunity offered by the United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO). This programme is specifically designed to equip young professionals from underrepresented countries with hands-on experience in international development, industrial policy, and sustainable economic transformation. Hosted at UNIDO Headquarters in […]

The post UNIDO Project Associate (IFTI) 2026 in Vienna: Paid UN Opportunity for Young Professionals from the Global South appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The UNIDO Project Associate – Industrial Futures Talent Initiative (IFTI) 2026 is a highly competitive early-career opportunity offered by the United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO). This programme is specifically designed to equip young professionals from underrepresented countries with hands-on experience in international development, industrial policy, and sustainable economic transformation.

Hosted at UNIDO Headquarters in Vienna, Austria, this role offers participants the chance to work within a global institution while gaining exposure to real-world projects that advance inclusive and sustainable industrial development.

Application Deadline

15 May 2026 (11:59 PM Vienna Time)

Applicants must submit their applications through the official UNIDO recruitment platform before the deadline.

About UNIDO and the IFTI Programme

The United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO) is a specialized UN agency that promotes:

  • Inclusive and sustainable industrial development
  • Poverty reduction and economic transformation
  • Environmental sustainability and climate action
  • Innovation and industrial competitiveness

The Industrial Futures Talent Initiative (IFTI) is a 24-month early-career programme developed to:

  • Strengthen UNIDO’s global talent pipeline
  • Increase representation from underrepresented Member States
  • Empower youth and promote gender equality
  • Provide professional experience within international development systems

The programme aligns with global priorities such as the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), particularly SDG 9 (industry, innovation, and infrastructure).

Position Overview

Role Title

Project Associate – Industrial Futures Talent Initiative (IFTI)

Location

Vienna, Austria (UNIDO Headquarters)

Contract Duration

  • 1 year (renewable for an additional year)

Intake Structure

  • Two intake rounds (2026 and 2027)
  • Approximately 6–8 candidates selected in total

What the Programme Offers

The UNIDO IFTI Programme provides a robust professional development experience combining technical exposure, mentorship, and real-world project work.

Key Benefits

  1. Competitive Salary
    • Approximately EUR 4,000 gross per month
  2. Additional Benefits
    • Air travel allowance (to and from Vienna)
    • 50% medical insurance subsidy
  3. Professional Development
    • Exposure to technical, economic, and industrial development
    • Hands-on experience in project design and management
  4. Mentorship and Support
    • Assigned mentor and buddy
    • Structured mentoring programme
  5. Global Work Environment
    • Work in a multicultural UN setting
    • Collaborate with international experts
  6. Career Growth
    • Build expertise in policy, research, and development
    • Strengthen your profile for international careers

Programme Components

Participants will engage in a comprehensive learning and working experience, including:

1. Technical and Industrial Exposure

  • Gain insights into industrial development trends
  • Work on sustainability, innovation, and economic competitiveness

2. Project Design and Management

  • Participate across the full project lifecycle
  • Learn planning, implementation, and evaluation processes

3. On-the-Job Training

  • Contribute to real UNIDO programmes
  • Develop practical professional skills

4. Mentoring Programme

  • Receive guidance from experienced UN professionals
  • Participate in peer learning and networking

Key Responsibilities

Selected candidates will support UNIDO programmes and projects by:

  • Conducting research on economic, industrial, and environmental topics
  • Preparing reports, presentations, and analytical documents
  • Supporting project design, monitoring, and evaluation
  • Maintaining documentation and institutional records
  • Assisting in organizing events, trainings, and missions
  • Contributing to policy and technical cooperation activities

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the UNIDO Project Associate (IFTI) Programme, applicants must meet the following requirements:

1. Age Requirement

  • Must be between 23 and 28 years old

2. Educational Qualification

  • Bachelor’s degree (minimum requirement)

3. Work Experience

  • At least 2 years of relevant professional experience

4. Technical Background (Desirable)

  • Experience in:
    • Industrial development
    • Sustainability
    • Economic competitiveness
    • Technical cooperation

5. Geographic Eligibility

Applicants must be nationals of eligible underrepresented UNIDO Member States (primarily from the Global South).

6. Language Requirement

  • Fluency in English (required)
  • Additional UN language (desirable)

7. Additional Requirements

  • Interest in sustainable industrial development
  • Experience in multicultural environments (advantage)

Diversity and Inclusion

UNIDO strongly encourages:

  • Women candidates to apply
  • Persons with disabilities to apply
  • Applicants from underrepresented countries

The organization is committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion in its workforce.

Required Competencies

Applicants are expected to demonstrate:

Core Values

  • Integrity
  • Professionalism
  • Respect for diversity

Key Competencies

  • Teamwork and collaboration
  • Results-oriented mindset
  • Effective communication
  • Innovation and problem-solving

Why You Should Apply

The UNIDO IFTI Programme is one of the most valuable entry points into the United Nations system for young professionals.

Key Reasons to Apply

  • Gain international work experience in the UN
  • Receive competitive remuneration and benefits
  • Build a global professional network
  • Develop expertise in industrial development and policy
  • Contribute to global sustainable development goals

How to Apply

Applicants must:

  1. Submit an online application via the UNIDO Recruitment System
  2. Ensure all eligibility criteria are met
  3. Prepare required documents and information
  4. Apply before the deadline

For more details about the UNIDO Project Associate (IFTI) 2026 in Vienna, visit HERE.

Discover more global Job opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post UNIDO Project Associate (IFTI) 2026 in Vienna: Paid UN Opportunity for Young Professionals from the Global South appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Postdoctoral Fellowship in International Law 2026 – Centre for International Law, NUS (Fully Funded Research Opportunity in Singapore)
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Centre for International Law (CIL) at the National University of Singapore is inviting applications for Postdoctoral Fellowship positions commencing in September 2026. This is a research-focused opportunity designed for early-career scholars aiming to pursue academic careers in international law. About the Centre for International Law (CIL) The Centre for International Law is a university-level […]

The post Postdoctoral Fellowship in International Law 2026 – Centre for International Law, NUS (Fully Funded Research Opportunity in Singapore) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Centre for International Law (CIL) at the National University of Singapore is inviting applications for Postdoctoral Fellowship positions commencing in September 2026. This is a research-focused opportunity designed for early-career scholars aiming to pursue academic careers in international law.

About the Centre for International Law (CIL)

The Centre for International Law is a university-level research centre dedicated to producing cutting-edge scholarship in international law. It plays a leading role in capacity-building across the Asia-Pacific region through:

  • Academic workshops and conferences
  • Professional training sessions
  • Research-driven programmes engaging academics, government officials, and private sector professionals

CIL is also strongly oriented toward supporting academic development and policy-relevant research in international law.

Fellowship Overview

The Postdoctoral Fellowship is a one-year appointment, with the possibility of renewal for a second year based on satisfactory performance during the first year.

  • Start date: September 2026
  • Duration: 1 year (renewable for a second year)
  • Location: Bukit Timah Campus, Singapore
  • Employee category: Research Staff
  • Posted on: 19 January 2026

The programme is directed by Dr. Nilufer Oral (Director of CIL and member of the International Law Commission), together with Professor Joseph Weiler, visiting professor at NUS.

Research Areas

Applications are open to candidates working in all areas of international law. However, special consideration will be given to research projects focused on:

  • The Asia-Pacific region
  • Climate change law
  • ASEAN law and policy

Fellows are encouraged to pursue research that is both academically rigorous and policy relevant.

Programme Objectives and Academic Environment

The fellowship is specifically designed to support scholars pursuing academic careers. Fellows will benefit from:

  • Individual academic mentoring
  • Workshops on research design and academic writing
  • Training in publication strategies and teaching methods
  • Guidance on supervision and methodological development

Fellows will also actively participate in the academic life of CIL, including presenting research, contributing to discussions, and engaging with visiting scholars.

Qualifications and Selection Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • A PhD in international law obtained within the last two years
    OR
    Doctoral candidates who will complete their dissertation by 31 July 2026
  • Demonstrated research excellence and originality in the doctoral dissertation
  • Strong academic potential and a well-developed postdoctoral research proposal

Required Submission Materials:

  • PhD dissertation (PDF)
  • Postdoctoral research proposal
  • One or two samples of published work

Letters of Recommendation:

  • Three confidential reference letters are required
  • Ideally, one should be from the PhD supervisor or examiner
  • Referees must send letters directly to: huong.tam@nus.edu.sg
  • References must assess:
    • Dissertation quality
    • Proposed research project
    • Academic potential

Application Procedure

Applications must be submitted online via the NUS career portal.

Deadlines:

  • Preferred deadline: 30 April 2026
  • Final deadline: 15 May 2026

Required Documents:

  • Personal statement (500–600 words) outlining motivation, career goals, and relevant background
  • Full curriculum vitae (including education, employment history, publications, awards, and honours)
  • PhD dissertation and doctoral certification
  • Research proposal (maximum 3000 words), including:
    • Research questions
    • Methodology
    • Expected contribution to the field
    • Anticipated outputs (e.g., journal articles, book manuscript)
    • Executive summary (maximum 600 words)
  • One or two writing samples
  • Three academic references (submitted separately)

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Programme Expectations

Selected Fellows are expected to:

  • Carry out the proposed research project during the fellowship period
  • Publish or secure acceptance for at least one or two peer-reviewed articles (or a book proposal with a reputable publisher)
  • Participate actively in workshops and academic discussions
  • Present research at conferences, including at NUS and international venues
  • Be in residence at NUS for the duration of the appointment (with limited approved travel for research and fieldwork)

Standard NUS leave policies apply.

Additional Information

  • Location: Bukit Timah Campus, Singapore
  • Organization: Centre for International Law, NUS
  • Job Category: Research Staff
  • Reference ID: 31488
  • Application status: Open until 15 May 2026
  • Notification: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted

How to Apply

Interested candidates should prepare all required documents and submit their application through the official NUS career portal before the deadline. Early submission is strongly encouraged due to preference for applications received by 30 April 2026.

APPLY HERE

Discover more similar opportunities Here. 

The post Postdoctoral Fellowship in International Law 2026 – Centre for International Law, NUS (Fully Funded Research Opportunity in Singapore) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: $10,000 UpGreyed Her Grant for Women-Led Startups in 2026
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Women entrepreneurs continue to face a significant funding gap—estimated at $1.9 trillion in 2026, up from $1.7 trillion the previous year. To help bridge this gap, Grey has launched its global UpGreyed Her initiative, offering $10,000 in equity-free grants to support women-led businesses across emerging markets and beyond. About the UpGreyed Her Initiative UpGreyed Her […]

The post Apply Now: $10,000 UpGreyed Her Grant for Women-Led Startups in 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Women entrepreneurs continue to face a significant funding gap—estimated at $1.9 trillion in 2026, up from $1.7 trillion the previous year. To help bridge this gap, Grey has launched its global UpGreyed Her initiative, offering $10,000 in equity-free grants to support women-led businesses across emerging markets and beyond.

About the UpGreyed Her Initiative

UpGreyed Her is a global program designed to scale women-led businesses by providing financial support and access to infrastructure that fuels growth. The initiative focuses on empowering women entrepreneurs who are leveraging technology to solve real-world problems.

Grey emphasizes that investing in women founders is not just about closing a gap—it’s about unlocking untapped potential and driving economic growth across regions. By supporting startups in cities like Lagos, São Paulo, and Jakarta, the initiative aims to multiply impact across entire economies.

Grant Details

A total of $10,000 in equity-free funding will be awarded to three outstanding women founders:

  • 1st Place: $5,000
  • 1st Runner-Up: $3,000
  • 2nd Runner-Up: $2,000

This funding comes with no equity requirements, allowing founders to grow their businesses freely.

Why You Should Apply

  • Gain equity-free funding to scale your business
  • Join a global network of women founders
  • Increase your visibility and credibility
  • Access support from a trusted financial platform

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the UpGreyed Her grant, applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • The business must be founded by a woman or have a female co-founder with at least 50% ownership
  • The company must have been operational for at least 3 years
  • The founder must be between 23 and 40 years old
  • Applicants must be citizens or residents of eligible countries across:
    • Africa
    • The Middle East
    • Southeast Asia
    • Europe
    • Latin America
    • The UK
    • The US
  • The business must use technology to solve real-world problems and be ready to scale

Read terms and conditions here.

Application Timeline

  • Applications Open: March 18, 2026
  • Deadline: April 20, 2026 (by 9:00 AM Pacific Time)

Early applications are strongly encouraged to avoid last-minute issues.

Transparent Evaluation Process

Applications will be reviewed by a panel of accomplished female judges with expertise in business, technology, and management. The evaluation process is designed to be fair, transparent, and focused on identifying high-impact ventures with strong growth potential.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should submit their applications through the official platform before the deadline. Be sure to review the full eligibility list and terms and conditions prior to applying.

APPLY HERE

Pro Tip

Focus your application on demonstrating measurable impact and scalability. Clearly show how your technology solves a real problem and how the grant will accelerate your next stage of growth.

Discover similar Grant opportunities Here.

The post Apply Now: $10,000 UpGreyed Her Grant for Women-Led Startups in 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: SIEF Call for Proposals 2026: Funding Opportunities for EdTech in Foundational Learning
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Strategic Impact Evaluation Fund (SIEF) has officially launched its eighth Call for Proposals, focusing on the role of education technology (EdTech) in improving children’s foundational learning. This initiative aims to support innovative, evidence-based solutions that can strengthen early learning outcomes worldwide. As digital tools continue to reshape education, this call emphasizes the need for […]

The post SIEF Call for Proposals 2026: Funding Opportunities for EdTech in Foundational Learning appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Strategic Impact Evaluation Fund (SIEF) has officially launched its eighth Call for Proposals, focusing on the role of education technology (EdTech) in improving children’s foundational learning. This initiative aims to support innovative, evidence-based solutions that can strengthen early learning outcomes worldwide.

As digital tools continue to reshape education, this call emphasizes the need for rigorous, policy-relevant research to determine what truly works in EdTech.

About the Program

Hosted by the World Bank, SIEF is dedicated to funding impact evaluations that generate reliable evidence for policymakers.

This latest call centers on “nimble evaluations”—rapid and cost-effective studies designed to test how technology can accelerate foundational learning and skills development among children.

Why This Matters

EdTech has gained global attention as a scalable and cost-effective solution for improving education. While some interventions have shown strong results, the broader evidence remains mixed, with many programs yielding only modest or no impact.

At the same time, emerging technologies—such as affordable digital devices and artificial intelligence—are expanding possibilities in education. This makes it increasingly important to evaluate which innovations truly deliver results, especially in foundational literacy and numeracy.

Key Focus Areas

SIEF Call 8 prioritizes:

  • Rapid, low-cost evaluations of EdTech interventions
  • Evidence generation to inform education policy
  • Solutions that improve foundational learning outcomes
  • Scalable and impactful digital education tools

Benefits of Applying

  • Access to research funding for EdTech evaluations
  • Opportunity to influence global education policy
  • Collaboration with the World Bank and leading researchers
  • Contribution to improving children’s foundational learning outcomes worldwide

Focus your proposal on clear, measurable outcomes and ensure your evaluation design is cost-effective and scalable. Strong proposals often combine innovation with practical policy relevance.

Eligibility & Application Process

There are two main application pathways:

1. Quick Assessment Form

  • For external researchers not currently collaborating with a World Bank Task Team Leader (TTL)
  • Deadline: April 22, 2026

2. Main Application Form

  • To be submitted by a World Bank Task Team Leader (TTL)
  • Deadline: May 19, 2026

Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the full call documents and ensure they meet all eligibility requirements before applying.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can access the following:

  • Call for proposals
  • Quick Assessment Form
  • Main Application Form
  • Submission links for both application stages

For additional inquiries, applicants can contact the SIEF team via email at siefimpact@worldbank.org with the subject line: “SIEF Call 8 query.”

Conclusion

The SIEF Call for Proposals 8 presents a unique opportunity for researchers and policymakers to advance evidence-based EdTech solutions. With a strong emphasis on rapid evaluation and real-world impact, this initiative aims to shape the future of foundational learning globally.

Discover similar opportunities Here.

The post SIEF Call for Proposals 2026: Funding Opportunities for EdTech in Foundational Learning appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
